name: DONUT

meta.data_required: 2 or 3 additive slices of one real total

use_when: exactly the pie use case, when the surrounding design system already standardizes on donuts
not_when: when angle reading matters (the hole removes the angle cue pies rely on)
dashboards stacking many small donuts (use CH-TAB-01 or CH-PTW-02)

data_shape.title: string, action title stating the dominant share
data_shape.eyebrow: string caps, rendered only when the DL chrome asks for it

scale.note: same construction as CH-PTW-03 with an annular cut

rules: the ring must stay thick (inner radius <= 62% of outer); thin rings are unreadable
the hole stays empty; a number in the hole belongs in the title
anti_patterns: more than 3 slices (the classic dashboard donut crime)
KPI number stuffed in the hole while the ring shows something else
thin progress rings pretending to be charts
qa: title states the dominant share with a number
